ops.title: cc-present main moves under you after every push (Guides bot auto-commit) — fetch+rebase before advancing the bookmark; gh run list --commit is unreliable here

ops.body:
In cc-present, `main` frequently moves between your commit and your push: the Guides workflow auto-commits "chore: re-render guides" to main after every push, and other sessions land work too. Consequences observed 2026-07-13:

- `ccx vcs ship` fails with `no bookmark to advance (heads(::@- & bookmarks()) matched none)` when main is no longer an ancestor. Recovery: `jj git fetch` → `jj rebase -s <change> -d main` → `jj bookmark move main --to <change>` → `jj git push` (a stale-base bookmark move refuses with "backwards or sideways").
- `gh run list --commit <sha>` often returns empty even when runs exist for that commit; find runs by `displayTitle` prefix (commit subject) or plain `gh run list`, then `gh run watch <id> --exit-status`.
- With parallel lanes (web + ios agents sharing one working copy), land each lane with a path-scoped `jj commit <paths>` so the other lane's in-progress edits stay in `@`.
- Path-scoping is not enough when a concurrent lane edits the SAME file: run `ccx vcs hunks <file>` on every shipped file while any concurrent lane is active and `--only-hunk` your own spans. Observed 2026-07-20: a whole-file ship of web/src/reduce.ts swept another stream's uncommitted annotation hunks into the commit without their events.ts counterpart, breaking tsc on CI; recovery was recommitting the clean file content and restoring the WIP into `@` afterwards.
- Never dispatch an implementation lane into a sibling repo's shared jj working copy, even one that is clean at dispatch time: another session's jj snapshot can clobber the lane's uncommitted edits mid-flight. Observed 2026-07-21 in cc-interact — a concurrent StoreSchema refactor's snapshot dropped a lane's config.go/server.go additions, leaving orphaned references and an uncompilable tree. Isolate in a `git worktree` off origin/main from the start (jj ignores it), capture the lane's diff as a patch early, and integrate with whatever main holds at ship time. Also: `ccx vcs ship` refuses when stale `worktree-wf_*` bookmarks make trunk ambiguous — pass `--bookmark main`.

- A concurrent session's local unpushed stack may be a STALE DUPLICATE of commits already on remote main under different SHAs (pushed from another machine). Rebasing that stack onto main conflicts on THEIR files — roll back (`jj op restore <pre-rebase op>`) rather than resolving their content, then ship your own work around it: exact-file `git diff <stack-tip> --binary -- <named files>` applied in a `git worktree add --detach` at main, committed there, `git push origin HEAD:main`. Name exact files, never directory globs — remote main may have gained changes inside your globs (observed 2026-07-21: CcPresentKit Comments/BoardStore landed remotely while local Kit edits sat uncommitted).
- If a commit hook edits/forces changes in the export worktree (SwiftLint function_body_length forced extracting a Swift helper), mirror the result byte-identically into the shared working copy afterward, or the next rebase of `@` conflicts on it.
- Local prek gotchas on yBook: the golangci hook dies env-wise (`GOTOOLCHAIN=local`, hook go 1.26.4 < go.mod's 1.26.5) — `--no-verify` is justified once build/test/lint already ran green by hand; SwiftLint's function_body_length is error-tier despite AGENTS.md's "warnings-only" claim.
- Vitest does NOT typecheck. After editing web/src, run `bun run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, noUncheckedIndexedAccess on) before shipping — CI's typecheck gate fails on narrowing patterns vitest happily executes (`Object.hasOwn` on a separate expression does not narrow an indexed access; bind the lookup and narrow the binding).

**Why:** ship's happy path failed twice in one session on these; the recovery steps aren't documented anywhere in the repo.

**How to apply:** before any bookmark advance or ship in this repo, `jj git fetch` first; after any push, expect main to have moved again by the next landing. Related: [[cc-present-dev-daemon-versioning]], [[cc-present-ios-client]].
